title: LGBTQ2+ Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > LGBTQ2+ Support Groups 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter LGBTQ2+ Youth Youth Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONTROVERSIAL COFFEE CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Queer owned and operated coffee shop that maintains a clothing closet with gently used, clean clothing and a small food pantry that is subject to availability. Also hosts a community night, providing a safe place for the LGBTQ2SIA+ community to gather and enjoy games and activities. Individuals may request a "Pay It Forward" meal, which covers the cost of a beverage and small food item. Accepts donations of gently used clean clothing, non-perishable food items, and a "Pay It Forward," meal, which covers the cost of a beverage and small food item. 111 Broadway Street Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Monday-Friday 7am-7pm Saturday-Sunday 7am-8pm -Queer Community Night: Monday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 739-0158 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES QUEER PEERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a gender and sexuality support group. Snacks provided. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Middle school-aged youth Hours: Monday 4:30pm-5:45pm Email (541) 745-4590 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SERVICE CENTER AND DROP IN QUEER PEERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a gender and sexuality support group. Snacks provided. 1025 Pacific Boulevard SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Thursday 5:30pm-7pm Email (541) 745-4590 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL AND TRANSGENDER NATIONAL HELP CENTER MODERATED YOUTH CHAT ROOMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weekly moderated groups for LGBTQIA+ youth and for transgender, pan-gender, and gender-fluid teens to talk in a safe space and to be able to fully express themselves without fear of being made to feel uncomfortable or unwelcome. Participants discuss concerns and issues and talk about personal victories. 2261 Market Street #296 San Francisco, CA 94114 Eligibility: LGBTQIA+ Chat Room: Individuals age 12-19 who identify as LGBTQIA+ Transgender Teen Chat Room: Individuals age 19 and younger who identify as transgender, pan-gender, gender fluid, gender expansive, or questioning Hours: LGBTQIA+ Teen Chat Room: Tuesday/Wednesday 4pm-7pm Transgender Teen Chat Room: Thursday/Friday 4pm-7pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL AND TRANSGENDER NATIONAL HELP CENTER LGBT NATIONAL COMING OUT SUPPORT HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe space to discuss concerns and questions regarding coming out (however that is defined by the caller) in a supportive and affirming space. 2261 Market Street #296 San Francisco, CA 94114 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-8pm, Saturday 9am-2pm Email (888) 688-5428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support services to the LGBTQ2SIA+ community and allies of the Lower Columbia region. Services include a community center, education opportunities, mentorship, and monthly support groups for adults, youth, and families. Limited financial assistance is available as funds allow for gender-affirming costs, name changes, hormone treatments, and other expenses approved on a case-by-case basis. Financial assistance is limited to once per year, with a $200 limit. 171 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Community Center walk in hours: Tuesday 2pm-5pm, Thursday noon-4pm. Other hours vary by service. Email (503) 468-5155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARIE EQUI CENTER LGBTQIA2S+ PEER SUPPORT DROP-IN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF LGBTQIA2S+ peer drop-in center provides resources, survival and harm reduction supplies, community connection, community health, and basic wound care. Offers peer support and case management to support community members in navigating systems of care, insurance systems, and direct connections to recovery and housing organizations. 4434 SE 25th Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Unhoused and low-income LGBTQIA2S+ community members Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed Monday, May 4, 2026-Friday, May 8, 2026 for staff development. Email (503) 459-2584 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers various support groups for different genders and questioning communities. Meetings occur virtually over Zoom at designated dates found on our link tree. 260 SW Madison Avenue Suite 110 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals who are transgender, non-binary, intersex, genderqueer, gender non-conforming, or intersex Hours: Varies by support group Email (541) 264-1973 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CENTRAL OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. PO Box 7462 Bend, OR 977087462 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (541) 316-0167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CLACKAMAS NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 501 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 344-5050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR COLUMBIA GORGE PRIDE ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe and confidential space for all members of LGBTQIA2s+ to gain support and skills, socialize, and meet others by promoting equity and inclusion. Hosts Pride parades, youth outreach, community partnerships, networking and educational opportunities. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Age 16-20 LGBTQIA2S+ teen Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEERGALAXY PEERGALAXY ONLINE COMMUNITY MEETINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides online community meetings to discuss various positive topics. These topics include life skills, self-reliance, creative expression, spiritual growth, wellness, peer support, and healing and recovery from mental health challenges, addictions, trauma, and grief. Meetings occur weekly and/or seasonally on the HeyPeers platform, usually on most days. Each meeting series typically lasts 10 weeks, although some may be longer or shorter, and each session generally lasts for 1 hour. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Visit Website More Details PFLAG LGBTQ2+ SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about support groups and specialized information and referral through local chapters of the organization. 1625 K Street NW Suite 700 Washington, DC 200065112 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as LGBTQ2+ and their friends and families Hours: Not applicable Email (202) 467-8180 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSPONDER EVENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mutual support meetings, social and special events. 440 Maxwell Road Eugene, OR 97404 Eligibility: Transgender and gender-diverse individuals and their allies Hours: Varies by event Email (541) 321-0872 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONTROVERSIAL COFFEE CLOTHING CLOSET JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES QUEER PEERS JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES QUEER PEERS LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER MARIE EQUI CENTER LGBTQIA2S+ PEER SUPPORT DROP-IN MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK SUPPORT GROUPS NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS NEXT DOOR COLUMBIA GORGE PRIDE ALLIANCE TRANSPONDER EVENTS
